architecture.summary: FPO (Feature-level constrained Preference Optimization) aligns large language models with human preferences by combining Direct Preference Optimization with feature-level constraints derived from pre-trained Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs). The system computes a preference loss using length-normalized log probability differences and an adaptive margin from a reference model, augmented with MSE constraints on sparse feature activations pooled across tokens. The reference model is computed offline and cached to avoid runtime memory overhead. Information flow: preference pairs → policy and reference forward passes → SAE sparse activations (pre-cached and computed offline) → MSE discrepancy between pooled top-k activations → combined loss with preference term.
trained_components: feature-level constraint weighting via λ hyperparameter

claims.statement: FPO achieves up to 5.08% absolute improvement in win rate compared to state-of-the-art baselines on AlpacaEval-2 and Arena-Hard benchmarks
FPO achieves approximately 17.6% GPU memory reduction compared to TDPO-2 during training
FPO achieves 64.1% training accuracy on UltraFeedback dataset with entropy of 1.68, compared to TDPO-2 at 64.2% accuracy and 1.68 entropy
On Gemma-2-2B, FPO achieves +0.5 score improvement on MT-Bench compared to SFT baseline
FPO leverages pre-trained Sparse Autoencoders to introduce feature-level constraints, where only a few dozen out of 16,000 features are active at any given time, enhancing computational efficiency
FPO uses offline reference caching to avoid loading the reference model during training, requiring only minimal I/O overhead to read pre-stored one-dimensional tensors at each step

claims.dataset: IFEval, MultiAlpaca, WildChat, Jailbreak Bench, AdvBench, Twitter Financial News Sentiment
claims.statement: FPO enables precise fine-grained control over specific model capabilities (instruction following, multilingual output, safety, sentiment) by selectively adjusting feature-level constraint weights (λ) on domain-specific features

FPO improvements on Gemma-2-9B are limited compared to Gemma-2-2B, attributed to larger models achieving more thorough feature decomposition with the same SAE width, filtering more noisy features and leading to less room for improvement
